title: Packing the Mesob: How to Protect Traditional Hand-Woven Wicker Dining Tables During Transit
excerpt: Packing mesob wicker dining tables: traditional hand-woven furniture protection, Ethiopian dining table moving, transit damage prevention packing guide.
content:
Packing the mesob: how to protect traditional hand-woven wicker dining tables during transit

The mesob is household altar and dining center—hand-woven wicker over wooden skeleton, wide brim vulnerable to crush and snap. Packing mesob for transit demands disassembly when possible, brim support, and no stack weight on woven rim—cultural furniture deserves craft-level care.

This guide protects traditional mesob and hand-woven wicker dining tables during moves.

Structure assessment

  • Solid wood base versus full wicker—handling differs.
  • Loose weave sections note before wrap—document photos.
  • Removable lid or cover if present—wrap separate.
  • Measure diameter—door diagonal clearance plan.

Packing technique

Cardboard disk cut brim diameter support underside; bubble brim edge; blanket wrap full circumference. Vertical orientation preferred—lean two padded walls truck mid-deck strapped. No flat stack with cartons on top.

Leg and base protection

Pad feet; strap legs if folding variant rare. Rope assist carry wide item two-person minimum—drag snaps wicker.

Climate and moisture

Breathable wrap not sealed plastic weeks—mold in weave. Rain day tarp over mesob only exterior—interior dry before seal truck.

Destination placement

Unpack before heavy furniture crowds room; center floor plan traditional if household observes—respect placement customs.

Insurance

Artisan receipt or appraisal; heritage irreplaceable—photo condition thorough.

Active Movers cultural furniture note

Disclose mesob dimensions at survey—brim support materials on truck.

Conclusion

Pack mesob with brim disks, vertical travel, two-person carry, and breathable wrap—hand-woven dining tables arrive intact when woven rim treated as fragile crown.
author: Active Movers Team
